item.name: Hatatate
item.description:
With a dorsal fin that makes it look like a standard-bearer, this saltwater fish is ready to charge bravely into a pitched battle under the sea.

[Suitable for printing on small canvases.]

fish.guide: Meaning “standard-bearer” in the Far Eastern tongue, the hatatate is identifiable by its prominent and forward-swept dorsal fin, a characteristic which earned it its name. Considered a symbol of bravery in Hingashi, it is favored among warrior families as an ornamental fish.
